name: Kyoto Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the imperial purple of the old capital
the purity and elegance of Kyoto's culture
harmony, peace, and the prefecture's bright future

description: A purple field charged at the centre with a six-fold radial white emblem resembling a snowflake or stylised flower, with a yellow centre.
history: Kyoto's prefectural symbol was selected by public competition and officially adopted on 2 November 1976. The flag, with the symbol on a purple field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same date.

features.symbolism: purple, the colour traditionally associated with the imperial court of Kyoto
a six-fold radial pattern stylising the snowflake or flower-petal, with a central yellow disc; designed to evoke the 'capital flower' (miyako-bana) and the cultural radiance of Kyoto.
